description: Validate all Solve preconditions (node indices, demand, arc shape, cost bound, nil capacities) before any pivot work.
commit: feat: add Solve input precondition validation
steps: Create validate.go in the repo root (SPDX BSL-1.0 header, package mcf).

Implement unexported `func validateSolveInputs(arcs []Arc, n, source, sink int, demand *uint256.Int) error` that returns a descriptive error (wrapped via fmt.Errorf with %w where appropriate) for each of the following violations. Each case returns a distinct error message identifying the offending index or value: (1) n < 2; (2) source or sink out of [0,n) or source == sink; (3) demand is nil or demand.IsZero(); (4) for each arc i: From or To outside [0,n); (5) self-loop (From == To); (6) Capacity is nil; (7) arc cost fails `arcCostWithinBound(cost, n)`.
The function must check node/demand bounds before iterating arcs so obviously-broken calls fail fast.
No mutation of the input slice; validation is read-only.

Create validate_test.go. Use a table-driven test TestValidateSolveInputs covering one successful case plus one failing case per distinct precondition (at least 7 failure cases). Each failure case asserts the returned error is non-nil and its message mentions the relevant field (e.g. `source`, `self-loop`, `capacity`, `cost`). The success case uses a 3-node graph with two real arcs and passes.
acceptance_criteria: validate.go exports no identifiers; all added symbols are package-private.
Every precondition in the spec's Numerical Preconditions section is covered by a distinct error branch.
validateSolveInputs returns nil for well-formed inputs.
TestValidateSolveInputs table covers at least 7 failure modes plus the success case; all assertions pass.
